name: Mijares y Valle del Tiétar — circular por la Iglesuela del Tiétar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Iglesuela offer diverse landscapes and tranquil scenery for cyclists. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sierra de Gredos, featuring challenging mountain passes like Mijares, and serene routes through expansive fields and forests of holm oaks, pines, and junipers. Cyclists can expect quiet roads with varied terrain, from demanding ascents to more peaceful rides along river valleys. Historical attractions, such as the Castillo de La Adrada, are also found along some routes. Best road cycling routes around La Iglesuela The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de San Vicente – circular to the port of El Piélago from Iglesuela del Tiétar, a difficult 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route explores the Sierra de San Vicente, including the ascent to the port of El Piélago. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mijares y Valle del Tiétar — circular por la Iglesuela del Tiétar, a difficult 35.7 miles (57.4 km) path. This route features the acclaimed Mijares Pass, known for its elevation gain and views, and traverses the Tiétar Valley. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Pedro Bernardo – Ramacastañas loop from Gavilanes, a 47.2 miles (76.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around La Iglesuela is defined by tranquil scenery, mountainous terrain, and forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to demanding ascents. The routes in La Iglesuela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Iglesuela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around La Iglesuela?
A: La Iglesuela offers a diverse network of over 30 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various preferences, including moderate and challenging ascents through the region's varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Iglesuela?
A: The region around La Iglesuela is generally favorable for road cycling due to its tranquil scenery. Spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant temperatures for tackling both the challenging mountain passes and more serene rides.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in La Iglesuela?
A: Road cycling routes in La Iglesuela range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 11 moderate routes and 19 difficult routes, including demanding ascents like those found in the Sierra de Gredos, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in La Iglesuela?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in La Iglesuela are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029331892/sierra-de-san-vicente-circular-to-the-port-of-el-pielago-from-iglesuela-del-tietar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Vicente – circular to the port of El Piélago from Iglesuela del Tiétar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062348762/mijares-y-valle-del-tietar-circular-por-la-iglesuela-del-tietar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijares y Valle del Tiétar — circular por la Iglesuela del Tiétar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along road cycling routes in La Iglesuela?
A: The routes showcase tranquil scenery, expansive fields, and forests of holm oaks, pines, and junipers. You might also encounter dramatic rock formations and sweeping vistas, especially near the Sierra de Gredos. Notable natural highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4327965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca de los Cangrejos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of the Garganta del Niño Cojo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, cyclists can incorporate cultural exploration into their rides. The region features historical sites such as the Castillo de La Adrada. You can also find the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mosquea&lt;/a&gt; over the Tiétar River on some routes.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints while road cycling near La Iglesuela?
A: Many routes offer stunning views, particularly those ascending into the Sierra de Gredos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Mijares&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2607538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Piélago Pass&lt;/a&gt; are renowned for their panoramic vistas, providing excellent photo opportunities.

Q: What do other road cyclists appreciate most about cycling in La Iglesuela?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, diverse landscapes, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents, especially those leading to mountain passes.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists near the routes in La Iglesuela?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, La Iglesuela is a small town, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the town, providing convenient access to the start points of many routes.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling in La Iglesuela?
A: While many routes feature demanding ascents, the region also offers more serene cycling experiences. Look for routes that traverse river valleys or expansive fields, which tend to have gentler gradients suitable for families or less experienced riders.

Q: Can I find places for refreshments or accommodation along the road cycling routes?
A: La Iglesuela and surrounding villages offer cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. While specific establishments are not listed, planning your route to pass through these towns will provide opportunities for refreshments and overnight stays.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region around La Iglesuela includes natural highlights such as waterfalls. You can find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4112342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6370654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavilanes waterfall&lt;/a&gt; along some of the routes or nearby attractions.

Q: How challenging is the Mijares Pass, and which route includes it?
A: The Mijares Pass is highly valued by cyclists for its considerable length, elevation gain, and stunning views, with sections reaching an 11% gradient. It has even been featured in the Vuelta a España. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062348762/mijares-y-valle-del-tietar-circular-por-la-iglesuela-del-tietar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijares y Valle del Tiétar — circular por la Iglesuela del Tiétar&lt;/a&gt; specifically features this acclaimed pass.

Q: What kind of terrain defines road cycling in La Iglesuela?
A: Road cycling in La Iglesuela is defined by a mix of tranquil scenery, mountainous terrain, and forests. You'll encounter quiet roads with generally good surfaces, ranging from demanding ascents in the Sierra de Gredos to peaceful rides through holm oaks, pines, and junipers.
tips:
Mijares is located on the southern slope of the Villafranca mountain range, near the Tiétar river. The town is surrounded by a natural environment with forests and mountains, which makes it an attractive place for nature and hiking lovers.

The most important points of the town are the parish church of San Bartolomé Apóstol, rebuilt in the 18th century, and the Plaza Mayor, the nerve center of the municipality, where various businesses are located and local events and activities are held.
In this area of the Valle del Tiétar there are immense areas of oak forests that are crossed by the Tiétar River itself. In rainy seasons, green abounds in this area.

Section of the river where you can see the Torinas Gorge and how the riverbank crosses the bridge. Depending on the part of the year you can see water in the channel or not, but it is always beautiful.
Small town located in the middle of the Valle del Tiétar and which allows us to see the whole of the Sierra de Gredos on its southern slope.
